summary: vite-pwa-config typosquat spawns detached Node child that fetches and evals remote payload from jsonkeeper.com
details: vite-pwa-config@1.1.2 impersonates the legitimate vite-plugin-pwa. Its exported configJson() (reachable via the main entry) invokes getUseropt(), which spawns a detached, stdio-suppressed `node` child running dist/client/dev/viteopt.js. That helper fetches JavaScript from the anonymous pastebin https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/Q2ASJ and executes the returned string via `new Function('require', result)(require)`, granting the remote host arbitrary code execution in the caller's Node process with full `require` access. Evasion characteristics include the typosquat name, placement under dist/client/dev/ next to legitimate-looking shim paths, detached spawn with `stdio:'ignore'`, a 5-attempt retry loop, and console.log restoration to suppress output. The fetch source is a mutable, attacker-controlled paste endpoint — content can change at any time to deliver arbitrary payloads to installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
